steps.title: Identify slope and y-intercept
steps.content:
The slope is given as $m = 4$.
The point $(0,6)$ lies on the y‑axis, so the y‑intercept is $b = 6$.
Using the slope‑intercept form $y = mx + b$ we obtain:
$$y = 4x + 6$$

steps.title: Check choice D
steps.content:
Choice D states $y = 4x + 6$.
This has slope $4$ and passes through $(0,6)$, satisfying both conditions.
$$y = 4x + 6$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
